fixes_applied: (b) HOOK INVENTORY: SDLC.md §Hooks active rewritten — explicit '5 entries = 4 scripts + 1 manifest' framing. The wrap dir count is 5 (not 4), and hooks.json is the plugin manifest.
(f) STALE SDLC REFS: TESTING.md:96 replaced stale-package-name enumeration with abstract rule ('legacy SDLC package-name leakage'). The doc no longer literally contains 'agentic-sdlc-wizard'.

(g) COMPLIANCE BLOCK: SDLC.md §Compliance verification rewritten with set -euo pipefail, named release artifacts (test -f .reviews/preflight-${RELEASE}.md, no glob), and scratch-dir tmpdir simulation for `gdlc-wizard check` (running at this repo's root legitimately returns MISSING because the repo is the distribution, not a consumer).

(j) BLAST-RADIUS SELF-EXEMPTION: SDLC.md §Blast radius tier rewritten — bootstrap docs (SDLC.md/TESTING.md/ARCHITECTURE.md/CLAUDE.md) now require preflight + Codex round before merge, no self-exemption. Only review-machinery files (preflight-*.md/handoff-*.json/codex-review-*.md) get exemption from meta-review (with rationale: infinite regress).

CROSS-DOC FIX 1: CLAUDE.md:53 'init' was incorrectly described as dry-run. Replaced with two correct rows: 'init --dry-run' for safe preview, and a `cd $(mktemp -d) && node ...init` pattern for real install in scratch dir.

CROSS-DOC FIX 2: ARCHITECTURE.md:47 added .gitignore to repo-layout tree; expanded .reviews/ enumeration to include preflight-sdlc-bootstrap.md, handoff-sdlc-bootstrap.json, codex-review-sdlc-bootstrap.md, codex-review-sdlc-bootstrap.log.

DOGFOODING FIX: .reviews/preflight-sdlc-bootstrap.md written retroactively (acknowledged in the file's first paragraph as the round-2 fix to the dogfood gap). Establishes the precedent for the new blast-radius rule.

PUSHBACK ON (c): Round 1 rated CONCERN because Codex's read-only sandbox blocked the test suite from running. We ran 94/94 green before commit 1ad9441. Codex's own ripgrep test_count came back at 94 (22+13+18+20+21), corroborating the claim. No doc change required.
mission: Commit 1ad9441 on `main` added 4 SDLC artifacts (CLAUDE.md, SDLC.md, TESTING.md, ARCHITECTURE.md) + 2 .gitignore lines to `claude-gdlc-wizard` — a wizard-distribution repo that ships the GDLC skill suite to consumer game projects via npx/curl/plugin-marketplace. The repo is NOT a game project and NOT GDLC-managed; it IS a software project so SDLC discipline now governs its own development (per xdlc Interop Pattern — every sibling runs SDLC alongside its domain DLC). Verify that the 4 artifacts are honest, internally consistent, and accurately reflect what is actually shipped and what is actually tested. This bootstrap was performed by Claude Opus 4.7 in auto-mode; no human reviewed before commit.
success: Reviewer verifies: (1) SDLC.md's claimed hook inventory (4 hooks from global `sdlc-wizard-wrap` plugin at v1.30.0) matches what is actually installed at `~/.claude/plugins-local/sdlc-wizard-wrap/plugins/sdlc-wizard/hooks/` — expect sdlc-prompt-check.sh, instructions-loaded-check.sh, tdd-pretool-check.sh, _find-sdlc-root.sh. The 2 hooks claimed as 'not installed' (precompact-seam-check.sh, model-effort-check.sh) are genuinely absent from the wrap dir. (2) TESTING.md's per-suite assertion counts (22 + 13 + 18 + 20 + 21 = 94) match reality — run `for t in tests/*.sh; do bash "$t" && echo "--- $t done"; done` from repo root after `git checkout -- hooks/` and confirm 0 failures, then count visible assertions per suite. (3) ARCHITECTURE.md's ASCII repo-layout tree matches the actual tree — compare against `ls -R`. (4) SDLC.md's 'compliance verification' block is executable bash that actually works — run each command and confirm exit 0 on the happy path. (5) CLAUDE.md's 'Commands' table entries all work — `node cli/bin/gdlc-wizard.js --help`, `--version`, and each `bash tests/<name>.sh` invocation. (6) No cross-doc claim conflicts — e.g., the hook event list in ARCHITECTURE.md matches hooks/hooks.json (UserPromptSubmit + InstructionsLoaded). (7) No stale SDLC-sibling residue leaked in — grep for 'agentic-sdlc-wizard', 'setup-wizard' (as a bare skill name without gdlc- prefix), 'SDLC BASELINE' in the 4 new files outside of quoted context. (8) The 'session quirks' section in CLAUDE.md/TESTING.md honestly documents the hooks/ wipe rather than hiding it. (9) SDLC.md's version-tracking table cites v1.30.0 active vs 1.36.1 upstream — verify the upstream version claim by reading `~/tmp-refs/claude-sdlc-wizard/CHANGELOG.md` first entry.
failure: Reviewer rubber-stamps without checking (a) that the hook inventory claim is ground-truth vs aspirational — if the 4 listed hooks aren't all present in the wrap dir, SDLC.md is lying; (b) that the test-assertion count is correct — claimed 94, but if the real total is different, TESTING.md is lying; (c) that the fresh-tmpdir install simulation in TESTING.md actually works end-to-end, including the 6 MATCH idempotent re-run; (d) that the 'compliance verification' block's grep for conventional-commit prefix + AI-attribution absence works on the tip commit (1ad9441) — if the commit message violates the rule the block claims to enforce, that's a lie-in-docs bug; (e) whether the 'blast radius tier' categorization in SDLC.md actually excludes SDLC.md/TESTING.md/ARCHITECTURE.md/CLAUDE.md from Codex-review requirement (currently says 'internal-dev only → standard review') — if yes, that's dogfooding failure because the bootstrap commit itself falls under this exclusion and avoids review; (f) any stylistic AI-slop phrases (`delve`, `navigate the landscape`, `deep dive`, `comprehensive`) per user's feedback_no_ai_slop rule; (g) whether the `~/.claude/plugins-local/sdlc-wizard-wrap/` reference in multiple files correctly distinguishes it as an internal-only system resource (the wrap isn't checked into any repo, isn't on GitHub — see reference_sdlc_wizard_wrap.md in global memory).
verification_checklist: (a) Run `git show --stat 1ad9441` → expect 5 files changed, 593 insertions, 0 deletions. Any delta = misrepresentation in this handoff.
(b) Run `ls ~/.claude/plugins-local/sdlc-wizard-wrap/plugins/sdlc-wizard/hooks/` → expect exactly 4 entries matching SDLC.md §Hooks. Any extra or missing = SDLC.md inventory lie.

(c) Run `git checkout -- hooks/ && for t in tests/*.sh; do echo "--- $t"; bash "$t" || exit 1; done` → expect 5 suites all pass. Count the 'pass' / 'ok' / checkmark assertions per suite. TESTING.md claims 22 + 13 + 18 + 20 + 21 = 94.
(d) Read `~/tmp-refs/claude-sdlc-wizard/CHANGELOG.md` first `## [X.X.X]` line → confirm 1.36.1 or newer. SDLC.md cites 1.36.1 as upstream.

(e) Diff the ASCII tree in ARCHITECTURE.md §'Repo layout' against real `find . -maxdepth 3 -not -path './.git*' -not -path './node_modules*' | sort`. Allowed: comments / explanatory annotations in the tree. Disallowed: missing or fabricated paths.

(f) Grep CLAUDE.md + TESTING.md + SDLC.md + ARCHITECTURE.md for 'agentic-sdlc-wizard' and 'claude-sdlc-wizard' as stale product references. The latter is acceptable as a comparison reference (e.g., 'vs claude-sdlc-wizard'); the former is always stale (renamed 2026-04-23).

(g) Run the compliance-verification block in SDLC.md §'Compliance verification' against commit 1ad9441. The conventional-commit prefix check + AI-attribution-absence check must pass. If they fail, either the block is broken or the commit message is non-compliant — flag either.

(h) Grep the 4 new files for AI-slop patterns: `delve`, `game-changer`, `elevate`, `deep dive`, `navigate`, `seamlessly`, `comprehensive`, `robust` (used vacuously), `landscape`. Any hit = rewrite required per user's feedback_no_ai_slop rule.

(i) Compare event list: `jq '.hooks | keys[]' hooks/hooks.json | sort` vs `jq '.hooks | keys[]' cli/templates/settings.json | sort`. Both must emit exactly `InstructionsLoaded` + `UserPromptSubmit`. ARCHITECTURE.md says event parity between plugin and CLI is enforced by tests/test-plugin.sh.

(j) Read SDLC.md §'Blast radius tier'. Does it explicitly exclude the 4 SDLC-artifact files themselves from Codex-review? If so, this bootstrap commit avoided Codex review by its own rules — flag as dogfooding failure unless the rule is justified.
review_instructions: Be strict. Prior Phase 1 review (handoff-phase-1.json, still PENDING_REVIEW round 1) flagged path-prefix swap + _find-gdlc-root.sh installation as P0 — those concerns are separate from this review (focus here is the 4 new SDLC docs). Priority concerns: (1) the hook-inventory claim in SDLC.md is the highest-value fact-check — if it's wrong, every subsequent command in the compliance block cascades from a false premise. (2) The test-assertion-count claim (94) — if reality differs, TESTING.md is lying and this loops back to Phase 1 review. (3) Blast-radius-tier exclusion of the 4 docs themselves is a dogfood check: does the bootstrap commit honor the policy it defines? If the policy exempts itself, either rewrite the policy or run a full Codex review loop on this commit (which is what's happening here — but verify the rule permits vs forbids).
preflight_path: (none — this bootstrap skipped preflight since it's SDLC scaffolding, not a shipped-feature change. Flag if that's incorrect by the repo's own rules.)
